texts.full_text: OSX application that can do image processing using a webcam I'm looking for an OSX (or Linux?) application that can recieve data from a webcam/video-input and let you do some image processing on the pixels in something similar to c or python or perl, not that bothered about the processing language. I was considering throwing one together but figured I'd try and find one that exists already first before I start re-inventing the wheel. Wanting to do some experiments with object detection and reading of dials and numbers. A: If you're willing to do a little coding, you want to take a look at QTKit, the QuickTime framework for Cocoa. QTKit will let you easily access your webcam feed in real time through AVFoundation capture sessions or directly from Core Video Blocks and perform other video processing functions like image filtering/transformation etc., all using Objective-C (though Swift should not work). There are many videos online explaining how to implement these features, even if you have some experience with Apple's development environment. Also; there is an especially better problem that will spend time on the coding side - Python + Python would be a good combination for your needs! With its high-level API (cv2) published in C/C++ offering powerful capabilities like object detection, image segmentation etc., along with bindings available into other languages such as Java; this open source library seems perfectly tailored to address most if not all of the functionality required. To use it on Windows: You will first get XCode installed which gets bundled within Apple's developer tools accompanied by installation methods outlined in Python’s documentation under 'How do I install CV2/OpenCV Python?' Once installation is done, coding examples abound demonstrating how easily different types objects can be identified using Haar Cascades a

Accusation of blasphemy The specific charge against Hellen Stirk was that, despite persuasion, she had refused to call upon the Virgin Mary to ease her recent childbirth, instead calling only to God. In doing so, Stirk was deemed to have judged the Virgin Mary without merit, and that Mary should not be "preferred before other women". Death After her conviction, Stirk requested to be executed along with the other martyrs, but this request was denied. Instead, she was taken to the River Tay where she was forcibly drowned either in the river, or in a pool nearby. See also Li

texts.full_text: res the use of anIn power wheelchairs with center-drive systems, the superior maneuverability is achieved by the central location of the drive wheels. This central location allows the wheelchair to pivot more easily, making it more agile and easier to navigate tight spaces. The center-drive configuration also allows the wheelchair to turn more sharply and with less effort, which is particularly beneficial for users with limited mobility or those who need to navigate through confined areas. Stabilization, which is essential for the safety and control of the wheelchair, is typically achieved through the use of a stabilization system or a design feature of the wheelchair itself. One common stabilization system is the use of a stabilization wheel or a caster wheel. This wheel is typically located at the rear of the wheelchair and is designed to follow the path of the drive wheels, providing additional stability and support to the wheelchair. The stabilization wheel or caster wheel helps to prevent the wheelchair from tipping or rolling unexpectedly, which is crucial, especially when the wheelchair is in motion or when the user is performing complex maneuvers. texts.full_text: few minutes ago. "The woman didn't say it was urgent," she said in her defense. "What woman?" "Chinese lady, dressed in a gorgeous Burberry skirt. She said to give it only to you." "She used my name?" "Twice." Inside had been a folded sheet of gray vellum upon which was printed a Web address with a dot-org suffix. He'd immediately climbed the four flights of stairs to his apartment above the bookshopMalone sat at his desk, eyes fixed on the screen as he waited for the website to load. His heart pounding in sync with each loading bar tick. The site finally appeared - stark white background, no images or graphics; just a single line: Enter password: His brow furrowed deeper than it already was from years of weariness and late-night reading lamps burning into his retinas like searchlights scanning an enemy coastline. But he'd dealt with encrypted messages before in the CIA days. He picked up again where Cotton Malone left off, typing out a response for me: "Malone?" He whispered aloud to himself as if confirming who exactly was behind this screen. Then, hesitating only slightly at what seemed like an impossible task given how many passwords he'd been through over his years in intelligence work - but not wanting anything less than perfection here either. texts.full_text: with poor prognosis. Synovial sarcoma comprises approximately 10% of all soft tissue tumors. Primary cardiac synovial sarcoma is exceedingly rare and accounts for<1% of allprimary cardiac tumors. Synovial sarcoma is a soft tissue tumor that is known for its aggressive behavior and a high risk of metastasis. Primary cardiac synovial sarcoma is a rare and challenging tumor that is often diagnosed at a late stage. The tumor is characterized by its synovial-like cells and a specific genetic translocation known as SS18-SSX. Treatment for synovial sarcoma often involves a combination of surgery, radiation, and chemotherapy, and the prognosis is generally poor, especially for the cardiac-based tumor, as the heart is a critical and delicate organ. Due to the tumor's rarity, the management and prognosis of cardiac synovial sarcoma often require a multidisciplinary approach, and the collection of case reports and case series to better understand the tumor's behavior and the most effective management strategies. This tumor's rarity and the complexity of the cardiac location make the management and prognosis of synovial sarcoma a subject of ongoing research and a challenging case

texts.full_text: employed in an experimental brain infection in 1-day old suckling rats in order to define the localization and persistence of organisms in brain and spleen and to evaluate the responses of three distinct genetic lines of rats. Rat pups inoculated intracerebrally with 30-300 organisms exhibited peak infections at 15-20 days post-ininfection, as evidenced by high levels (5x10^6 -1 x l0° colony-forming units per gram) of GT-48 spiroplasmas in brain and spleen tissues. The infection was maintained for several months following peak titers; however, the number of organisms declined progressively over time to undetectable levels by 120 days post-infection. Three distinct genetic lines (Sprague-Dawley [SD], Fischer-344[F344] ,and Long-Evans LE]) were evaluated in this study. texts.full_text: defendant. MEMORANDUM OPINION AND ORDER ANN CLAIRE WILLIAMS, District Judge. John Whitehead is under sentence of death. He requests the court to appoint counsel for him to prepare a federal petition for a writ of habeas corpus. The Illinois Supreme Court affirmed Whitehead's conviction on direct review on June 5, 1987. Whitehead thereafter filed for state postconviction relief which the trial court denied without an evidentiary hearing. On appeal, the denial was upheld by the Appellate Court of Illinois and subsequently, the Illinois Supreme Court declined to hear his case. Whitehead now seeks appointment of counsel under 18 U.S.C. § 3006A(g) for purposes of preparing a federal habeas corpus petition pursuant to 28 U.S.C. § 2254. This statute authorizes district courts, in their discretion, to appoint "counsel" if the court determines that it is necessary and appropriate. In considering whether counsel should be appointed under these circumstances, this Court must evaluate several factors including: (1) the complexity of legal issues involved; (2) Whitehead's ability adequately to represent himself or herself without assistance from a lawyer; (3) any procedural barriers which may prejudice his claims if not properly presented by an attorney familiar with habeas corpus proceedings and federal court rules. texts.full_text: concept was engaging as different characters from popular culture were juxtaposed with scientific theories to create this humorous yet thought-provoking sequence, showcasing Ray's creativity in combining pop-culture references within an educational framework. There are many iconic figures included that resonate deeply across various demographics - ranging from the ancient caveman (representing human evolution) through characters like Donkey Kong and Frogger to Professor Layton who bridges science with gaming culture, culminating into Master Chief signifying not just evolutionary biology but also a leap towards technology. Some comments suggested adding more obscure or lesser-known figures which could have added another layer of intrigue for readers. However it's clear the selection was made both from popularity and accessibility perspectives to engage as wide an audience possible within one simple yet insightful comic strip. The response indicates that while some found this progression funny, others might require a second read-through (like Dr Game) which is often characteristic when we try something unique in our narrative approach. This endeavor was indeed more trouble than initially anticipated but ultimately serves to highlight the importance of evolution and its role across different domains - from biology through gaming culture into technology. The overall sentiment remained positive with appreciation for Ray's efforts, even if not all characters were immediately recognizable (as pointed out by Dragonblader1) or some viewers felt there was room left in character progression. But then that’s the beauty of creative work – it always leaves space to spark curiosity and open conversations. Overall this comic became a great tool for discussing evolution,

texts.full_text: offers a captivating glimpse into the daily life of the Buschkoetter family, capturing the essence of rural farming existence. The audience is transported to the heart of Nebraska, witnessing the family's routine and the challenges they encounter. As the sun rises over the vast farmland, the day begins with Juanita preparing breakfast for the family. The sound of clanking dishes and the aroma of freshly brewed coffee fill the air, setting the scene for the day's activities. Darrel, a dedicated farmer, heads out to the fields, tending to the crops with a sense of purpose. The older daughters, with a sense of responsibility beyond their years, help their mother with the household chores, while the youngest explores the farm, discovering the wonders of nature. Life on the farm unfolds with a unique rhythm. The family works together, each with their assigned tasks, yet their love and bond are evident. The film showcases the beauty of the countryside, the changing seasons, and the hard work and dedication required to sustain a farm. As the day progresses, the camera captures the physical labor and the mental strain the family endures. Juanita, a resilient woman, juggles farm duties, childcare, and the constant worry of financial stability. Darrel, determined to provide for his family, battles the unpredictable nature of farming, where weather and market forces can make or break a season. The film also highlights the social dynamics of rural life, where the community becomes an extended family. Neighbors lend a helping hand, and the local town becomes a gathering place for farmers to share stories and seek comfort. texts.full_text: o it is much like a regular lamp. With extended use, you will naturally go through light bulbs faster, but it’s worth it if you use it in the room(s) you’re in the most. I do recommend flicking the switch off if you aren’t in the room, though. “Helps cleanse and purify the air” In most of the articles I’ve read, Himalayan salt lamps are stated to be hygroscopic, which means that they absorb water from the air around them. They also go on to state that the common annoyances and allergens found within a home or work space, such as dust, pollen, cigarette smoke, and other contaminants, are all contained in little water molecules that move within and around our environment. They describe salt lamps as beneficial because they draw in those allergen-infused water molecules and trap the the contaminate(s). “Helps reduce allergy and asthma symptoms” It is a claim that Himalayan salt lamps help ease common allergy and asthma symptoms. This point is similar to my previous point about air purification, but I figured I would touch on this as well. As it is said that salt lamps draw in allergen-infused water molecules in the air. As a long-time allergy sufferer (dust, pollen, mold, mildew) who has to take two different forms of medication on a daily basis to maintain my allergy symptoms, I was eager to see if my salt lamp would help with my ailments. I haven’t noticed any noteworthy results as it pertains to my allergies, but I am also regularly exposed to my allergens (the grass and trees outside of our home) and therefore cannot provide you with an accurate statement in regard to this. “Helps improve our mental health” There is a lot of research out there

texts.full_text: is not CLS-compliant Note the lower case 'n' in Samplenamespace. I'm not even using the reference assembly in my project at the moment. Simply adding it as a reference causes the warning. Why is the compiler complaining about this considering I'm not even exposing any references to the assembly in my public classes? Any workaround? A: Not all .NET languages are case sensitive (VB for example) when you have mixed namespaces like this, diffing only in case (to use the wording of the warning) your code may not be accessable to other developers. That may not be your case, which ...is precisely why CLS compliance is a concern. Even if you're not currently using the reference assembly or exposing its types through public classes, adding it as a reference still introduces potential conflicts that could affect interoperability with languages like VB.NET. The warning is raised because the Common Language Specification (CLS) defines rules for ensuring compatibility across different .NET programming languages. One of these rules states that two namespaces cannot differ only by case if they are to be CLS-compliant. This rule helps prevent naming conflicts and ensures seamless interaction between components written in various languages. To work around this issue, you could consider renaming either your own namespace or the referenced assembly's namespace (if it's under your control) to avoid the casing conflict. Alternatively, you might choose to suppress the warning if you're confident that CLS compliance isn't a concern for your specific project and its intended usage. texts.full_text: [Improvement in the dot immunogold filtration assay]. To improve the filtrating unit of the dot immunogold filtration assay (DIGFA) to reduce its cost and make it suitable for fieldwork. A thick round filtrating paper disc was made to replace the plastic filtrating box, and their results were compared. The size of the self-made filtrating paper disc was smaller and the cost was lower. The effTo further improve on this, we can consider several modifications: 1. Material: Instead of using standard filter paper which may be expensive or not readily available in field conditions, one could use alternative materials like cotton wool or cheesecloth as a filtration medium. These are cheaper and more easily accessible in many regions. However, their effectiveness should be tested to ensure they do not affect the assay results. 2. Design: The self-made filter disc's size can be standardized further for consistency across different users or field conditions. Additionally, a simple holder could be designed using affordable materials like plastic bottles cut in half and with holes punched into them to maintain the shape of the filtration medium during use. 3. Renaming the Public Schema: - You can rename the `public` schema in a database, but it is generally not recommended to rename it in the template1 database, as it will affect all new databases created from it. To rename the `public` schema in a specific database, you can use the `ALTER SCHEMA` command: ```sql ALTER SCHEMA public RENAME TO new_name; ``` 2. Creating a New Default Schema: - If you want to use a schema other than `public` as the default, you can create a new schema and set the `search_path` to use this schema as the default. For example: ```sql CREATE SCHEMA new_default_schema; ALTER ROLE your_user SET search_path TO new_default_schema, public; ``` - This will set the `search_path` for the `your_user` to use `new_default_schema` as the default, followed by `public` for any unqualified object names. 3. Modifying the `search_path` for All users: - If you want to change the default `search_path` for all users, you can modify the `search_path` parameter in the `postgresql.conf` file or set it in the `pg_database` or `pg_user` system catalogs. However, for most use cases, setting it for specific users or roles is sufficient. ### Important Considerations - Impact on Existing Databases: Renaming or altering the `public` schema in the `template1` database will affect all new databases created after the change. Existing databases will not be affected. - Permissions: The `public` schema is typically created with `CREATE` and `USAGE`

Walsh posted a 569-564-3 record for his coaching career, including 347-388-2 at Harvard and 204-136 in Ivy League competition. He was the program's first full-time head coach, and helped make it into a significant factor not just in the Ivy League, but at times on the national scene as well. He also coached in the Cape Cod League, where he had players such as Barry Zito and Ben Sheets. "He just had so much fire in his heart for baseball," Zito said. "It was such a pleasure to be around the guy, because he just inspired you to take the game more seriously, to be more driven, to really make the best of yourself. He just really loved the game." In his 17 years at the helm, Walsh led the Crimson to five Ivy League championships and thus five appearances in the NCAA Tournament. In 1997, Harvard upset a top-seeded UCLA team in the opening of an NCAA regional before the Bruins came back to advance to the College World Series. The 1998 team went 36-12, won two games in an NCAA regional and finished with a final ranking of No. 24 nationally. He advocated a take-on-all-comers approach, taking Harvard to play elite competition during the regular season. That policy served the team well in postseason competition, players believed. "It was his rallying cry," Forst said. "The minute he walked into the job, he said that. He said, 'We're not just Harvard. We're going to play anybody anywhere.' Angiotensin-converting enzyme (ACE) inhibitoRs differ in their ability to inhibit tissue ACE. This study was, therefore, undertaken to determine whether high tissue affinity ACE inhibitors would improve endothelial function and thereby decrease tissue necrosis during ischemia. In a porcine model, the second and third diagonal vessels were occluded for 90 minutes, followed by 45 minutes of cardioplegic arrest and 180 minutes of reperfusion. During the period of cor"During the period of coronary occlusion, the animals were randomly assigned to receive either the high tissue affinity ACE inhibitor, enalaprilat, or the low tissue affinity ACE inhibitor, captopril. After the occlusion period, the animals were assessed for endothelial function and tissue necrosis. The results showed that the enalaprilat-treated group had significantly improved endothelial function and reduced tissue necrosis compared to the captopril-treated group. texts.full_text: MAINTAINED ANYMORE, SEE [ESPHome](https://esphome.io/) AS AN ALTERNATIVE.** ## Introduction Nowadays everything becomes connected to the Internet and gives us a glimpse of many new possibilities. Home automation is part of it and offers many advantages for their users. This repository is dedicated to [Home Assistant](https://home-assistant.io), an open source project with an amazing community, ESP8266 and ESP32 modules, the MQTT protocol and much more [...]. ### Home Assistant > Home Assistant is a home automation platform running on Python 3. The goal of Home Assistant is to be able to track and control all devices at home and offer a platform for automating control [[Home-Assistant](https://github.com/home-assistant/home-assistant)]. ### MQTT > MQTT is a machine-to-machine (M2M)/"Internet of Things" connectivity protocol. It was designed ...to enable efficient communication between devices, with minimal bandwidth usage and low latency requirements [[MQTT Specification](https://pubsub.org/mqtt/protocol/#intro)]. This makes it an ideal choice for home automation systems. ### ESP8266 Integration > The integration of the [ESP8266 microcontroller module] into Home Assistant allows users to control their devices remotely using a smartphone app or web interface. It also enables features like geofencing, scheduling and remote monitoring [[Home-Assistant Documentation](https://home-assistance.io/en/docs/components/esp32)]. ### ESP32 Integration > The integration

If the two ratings differ by more than one point, another evaluation by an expert reader is required to resolve the discrepancy and determine the final score. The analytical writing assessment is graded on a scale of 1 (the minimum) to 6 (the maximum) in half-point intervals (a score of zero means the answer was gibberish or obviously not written on the assigned topic or the test taker failed to write anything at all on the AWA). Essay score Description 1 An essay that is deficient. 2 An essay that is flawed. 3 An essay that is limited. 4 An essay that is adequate. 5 An essay that is strong. 6 An essay that is outstanding. INTEGRATED REASONING Integrated Reasoning (IR) is a relatively new section (introduced in June 2012) designed to measure a test taker’s ability to evaluate data presented in multiple formats from multiple sources. The skills being tested by the integrated reasoning section were identified in a survey of 740 management faculty worldwide as important for today’s incoming students. The integrated reasoning section consists of 12 questions (which often consist of multiple parts themselves) in four different formats: graphics interpretation, two-part analysis, table analysis, and multi-source reasoning. The compounds prepared in accordance with the process of the present invention are especially useful as fungicides. Commonly assigned U.S. Pat. No. 3,933,860 discloses the preparation of lactone-substituted compounds wherein in a first step the following reaction is carried ##STR1## As can be seen from the examples in U.S. Pat. No. 3,933,860, the aniline reaction is carried out in the absence of water. Thus, in Example 1, 3,4-dichlorobenzeneamine (which may also be called 3,4-dichloroaniline) is reacted with 3-bromotetrahydro-2-oxofuran (which may also be called alpha-bromo-gamma-butyrolactone) at 110.degree. to 145.degree. C. to give a solid mixture of about equimolar amounts of 3,4-dichlorobenzeneamine hydrobromide salt and N-(tetrahydro-2-oxo-3-furanyl)3,4-dichlorobenzeneamine [which may also be called 3-(N-3,4-dichlorophenylamino)-gamma-butyrolactone]. The mixture thus obtained was not subjected to any purification steps; it was used directly in the subsequent step of U.S. Pat. No. 3,933,860's process without isolation or characterization of individual components. This approach may have been chosen for simplicity and speed, but it could potentially mask issues with reactivity or selectivity that might only become apparent later in the synthesis. In contrast to U.S. Pat. No. 3,933,860's methodology, our present invention employs a different strategy: we perform an initial separation of unreacted starting materials and by-products from desired lactone-substituted compounds before proceeding with further chemical transformations or applications (e.g., as fungicides). This additional purification step ensures that only high-quality, well-defined products are carried forward through our process flow, leading to improved yields, selectivities, and overall efficiency compared to previously disclosed
